erc20_token_assumptions.assumption: Asset token returns correct balances (not rebasing)
erc20_token_assumptions.risk: Rebasing tokens would cause share/asset accounting drift in ERC-4626

erc20_token_assumptions.assumption: Asset token does not charge fees on transfer
erc20_token_assumptions.risk: Fee-on-transfer tokens: deposited amount != received amount; vault under-collateralized

erc20_token_assumptions.assumption: Asset token does not have ERC-777-style receive hooks
erc20_token_assumptions.risk: Reentrancy during deposit/withdrawal via token callback
erc20_token_assumptions.test: Test with ERC-777-compatible token; ReentrancyGuard on Chamber should protect

erc20_token_assumptions.risk: Non-reverting transfers (e.g., early USDT) could succeed but transfer 0
erc20_token_assumptions.test: SafeERC20 not used in Chamber — verify all paths handle non-reverting tokens
erc20_token_assumptions.note: ERC-4626 deposit/withdraw paths use the asset token's own transfer calls via _asset.safeTransfer in OZ implementation — verify OZ uses SafeERC20 internally

erc721_nft_assumptions.assumption: NFT ownerOf reverts for burned/non-existent tokenIds
erc721_nft_assumptions.risk: If ownerOf returns address(0) instead of reverting, the try/catch in delegate() and _isDirector() succeeds, allowing delegation to non-owned tokens
erc721_nft_assumptions.test: Test with non-standard NFT that returns address(0) for burned tokens

erc721_nft_assumptions.assumption: NFT ownerOf is non-reentrant / does not call back into Chamber
erc721_nft_assumptions.risk: Malicious NFT could re-enter during delegate(); state partially updated
erc721_nft_assumptions.test: Test with malicious re-entering NFT; full transaction should revert
erc721_nft_assumptions.note: Reentrancy via ownerOf is limited because msg.sender changes in nested call

erc4626_vault.risk: Attacker needs to donate 1000x more capital — economically infeasible

erc4626_vault.assumption: Chamber shares (ERC-20) can be freely transferred between depositors
erc4626_vault.risk: Transfers bypass delegation lock only if _update() check passes

proxy_and_registry.assumption: TransparentUpgradeableProxy correctly isolates admin and user calls
proxy_and_registry.risk: Admin selector clash could prevent non-admin calls (OZ handles this via TRANSPARENT proxy pattern)
proxy_and_registry.status: SAFE — OZ Transparent proxy correctly routes calls
